assertions.title: Regulatory mapping UI display

steps.observed: Report page loaded successfully with 28 findings

steps.action: Inspect FindingCard.tsx component code for RegulationBadge component
steps.expected: Component renders regulatory badges with distinct visual styling
steps.observed: RegulationBadge component exists with colored pills using REGULATION_INFO (FTC=orange, GDPR=blue, DSA=purple, CPRA=green), rendered in a regulation-row div distinct from action-row pills

steps.expected: Regulatory badges have distinct styling from other pills
steps.observed: CSS defines .regulation-pill with border-radius: 999px, font-size: 11px, font-weight: 600, uppercase text-transform, inline colored backgrounds per regulation. .regulation-row has flex layout with 6px gap and 8px margin. Clearly distinct from .signal-pill styling

steps.action: Check API data and DOM for regulation-row elements
steps.expected: Regulatory badges rendered when data present
steps.observed: 0 regulation-row elements rendered because all 28 findings have empty regulatory_categories arrays (seeded before feature was wired). Component correctly conditionally renders: {regulatoryCategories.length > 0 && <div className='regulation-row'>...}

steps.action: Verify ReportPage.tsx has Regulatory Summary section
steps.expected: Regulatory summary section exists in report page
steps.observed: ReportPage includes a full 'Regulatory summary' section that renders regulatory-grid cards with color-coded regulation info (FTC/GDPR/DSA/CPRA) when findings have regulatory_categories. Correctly hidden when no regulatory data exists

codeInspection: FindingCard.tsx: RegulationBadge component renders colored pills per REGULATION_INFO with backgroundColor, color, borderColor from taxonomy constants
FindingCard.tsx: regulation-row div conditionally rendered when regulatoryCategories.length > 0
ReportPage.tsx: Regulatory summary section with regulatory-grid cards per regulation
index.css: .regulation-pill has distinct styling (11px font, 600 weight, uppercase, colored per regulation)
taxonomy.ts: REGULATION_INFO defines FTC (#f97316), GDPR (#3b82f6), DSA (#8b5cf6), CPRA (#10b981)

assertions.issues: No regulatory badges visible in UI because existing findings have empty regulatory_categories arrays (data was seeded before the feature pipeline was wired in). The frontend components are correctly implemented and would display badges when data is populated. This is a data seeding issue, not a UI bug.

steps.action: Navigate to report page and scroll to FindingCards
steps.observed: All 28 FindingCards display confidence percentage pills (e.g., '97% Confidence', '95% Confidence')

steps.expected: Confidence has visual indicator distinct from other pills
steps.observed: CSS .confidence-pill has distinct green styling: background rgba(92,212,169,0.15), color #15803d, borderColor rgba(92,212,169,0.3) — clearly distinct from severity and pattern family pills

steps.expected: All finding cards have confidence pills
steps.observed: 28 elements with class .confidence-pill found in DOM, matching all 28 findings
Two different confidence levels visible: 97% (most findings) and 95% (urgency findings). formatConfidenceAsPercentage function correctly converts 0.97 → '97%' and 0.95 → '95%'

assertions.title: False-positive suppression UI

steps.action: Check API data for suppressed findings
steps.observed: All 28 findings have suppressed=false. No currently suppressed findings in dataset.

steps.action: Verify executive summary suppression count display
steps.observed: Finding count card shows '28' with subtitle 'Evidence-backed trust and compliance concerns' (the default text when suppressedCount is 0). Code correctly implements: when suppressedCount > 0, shows 'X confirmed (Y suppressed)' text instead

steps.expected: Suppressed findings get distinct visual treatment
steps.observed: Code implements: isSuppressed = finding.suppressed ?? false; article gets 'finding-card-suppressed' class when suppressed; 'Likely false positive' pill rendered via suppressed-pill class

.finding-card-suppressed has opacity: 0.7, muted background rgba(248,251,255,0.75), subtle border. .finding-card-suppressed .finding-title has color: var(--muted). .suppressed-pill has gray styling (color: #6b7280)

steps.expected: No suppressed elements visible (no suppressed data)
steps.observed: 0 elements with .finding-card-suppressed class, as expected with 0 suppressed findings

codeInspection: FindingCard.tsx line: {isSuppressed && Likely false positive}
ReportPage.tsx: suppressedCount = findings.filter(f => f.suppressed).length; shows 'X confirmed (Y suppressed)' when > 0

assertions.issues: No findings are currently suppressed in the dataset so the suppression UI cannot be demonstrated visually. However, the code implementation is verified correct through component and CSS inspection. The executive summary correctly handles both cases (0 suppressed = default text, >0 suppressed = count text).

assertions.title: Evidence source labels

steps.action: Navigate to report page and view FindingCards
steps.observed: All 28 FindingCards display 'Evidence' as the source label in a distinct source-pill

steps.action: Check API data for source label values
steps.observed: evidence_payload.source is not populated (N/A) for existing findings. getEvidenceTypeLabel returns 'Evidence' as default fallback when source is undefined

steps.expected: Source label has distinct visual indicator
steps.observed: CSS .source-pill has purple styling: background rgba(139,92,246,0.12), color #7c3aed, borderColor rgba(139,92,246,0.3) — distinct from confidence and pattern pills

steps.expected: All finding cards have source pills
steps.observed: 28 elements with class .source-pill found in DOM

steps.expected: Function maps source values to human-readable labels
steps.observed: Function maps: nova_ai → 'Nova AI evidence', heuristic → 'Heuristic detection', mock → 'Simulated', rule_based → 'Rule-based'. Falls back to 'Evidence' when source is undefined. Handles hyphen-to-underscore normalization

codeInspection: taxonomy.ts: EVIDENCE_TYPE_LABELS = { nova_ai: 'Nova AI evidence', heuristic: 'Heuristic detection', mock: 'Simulated', rule_based: 'Rule-based' }
taxonomy.ts: getEvidenceTypeLabel returns 'Evidence' when source is undefined

assertions.issues: Current findings don't have evidence_payload.source populated, so all cards show the default 'Evidence' label. When source is populated (e.g., 'nova_ai'), the label would display as 'Nova AI evidence'. The component and label mapping are correctly implemented.

frictions.description: Existing seeded findings have empty regulatory_categories arrays and missing evidence_payload.source values because they were seeded before these features were wired into the audit pipeline. This prevents visual demonstration of regulatory badges and varied source labels in the UI.
frictions.resolution: Supplemented UI testing with code inspection of FindingCard.tsx, ReportPage.tsx, taxonomy.ts, and index.css to verify the component implementations are correct. DOM queries confirmed the structural elements (confidence-pill, source-pill) are rendered.

frictions.description: No findings have suppressed=true in the dataset, preventing visual demonstration of the suppression UI styling.
frictions.resolution: Verified the implementation through code inspection: FindingCard.tsx correctly applies 'finding-card-suppressed' class and 'Likely false positive' badge, CSS defines muted styling, and ReportPage.tsx executive summary displays suppression counts.

frictions.description: Report page has very long screenshot timeline that requires extensive scrolling (5000+ pixels) to reach the Findings and evidence section.
frictions.resolution: Used JavaScript eval to scroll directly to .finding-card elements instead of manual scrolling.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: all 4 passed. VAL-ADV-004 (confidence) and VAL-ADV-008 (source labels) are fully visible in the UI with 28 confidence pills showing 95-97% and 28 source pills showing 'Evidence'. VAL-ADV-002 (regulatory badges) and VAL-ADV-006 (suppression UI) have correct component implementations verified through code inspection but cannot be visually demonstrated because existing findings lack regulatory_categories data and none are suppressed.
